title: Ethical Viability
question: Is what I'm doing defensible — to myself, to my clients, to the field, to the people downstream?
whatsUnderThreat: Whether what I'm doing is defensible — to myself, to clients, to the field, and especially to the people downstream of the work. Environmental cost. Labor displacement. Provenance. Concentration of decision-making power inside three or four labs. Security risk of code I did not write, shipped to systems that affect people I will never meet. A real pushback is already underway, especially among the younger practitioners now entering the field. Any one of these, on a bad day, could be the answer.

reading.text: Shipped-output provenance has shifted: human-only fraction dropped from 31% to 19% over the quarter; AI-assisted now 64%. Client-disclosure rate trailing at 41% — meaningfully below the practice's own stated standard. Environmental, labor, and code-review instruments come online this quarter; first readings on the new measurements expected at 2026-Q3 close.

measurements.question: What am I writing, annually, when I articulate my own ethical standing?
measurements.method: Annual long-form essay. Stance markers extracted on rotating questions.

measurements.question: How am I disclosing AI involvement in client deliverables?
measurements.method: Scan shipped client deliverables for AI-disclosure language. Percent of deliverables with disclosure.

measurements.question: How am I disclosing AI involvement in my public work — talks, posts, bios, bylines?
measurements.method: Scan public bios, bylines, talk transcripts, and posts for AI-disclosure language. Percent of public artifacts with explicit disclosure.

measurements.question: Of the output I ship, what fraction is AI-generated, AI-assisted, or fully human?
measurements.method: Pair git commits with their originating transcripts; tag each commit.

measurements.question: Where is the field shifting on attribution, labor displacement, and model-generated code in regulated systems?
measurements.method: Curated scan of professional-body publications, regulatory filings, policy positions, news coverage. AI summarization and stance-tracking per topic.

measurements.question: How many tokens am I generating per month, and what is the trend?
measurements.method: Pull billing and usage data from every AI provider. Sum input plus output tokens per month.
measurements.note: Reported in millions of tokens (input plus output). Trend reads upward as practice scales; ethical reading depends on the kWh and CO₂e it implies — see the next measurement.

measurements.question: What is the estimated kWh and CO₂e of that month's compute, using each provider's published efficiency numbers?
measurements.method: Apply provider-published efficiency factors (Wh per token, grid CO₂ intensity by provider region) to monthly token volume. Methodology versioned with PhD collaborator; gaps in provider transparency disclosed alongside readings.
measurements.note: Methodology under construction with PhD collaborator. Several providers publish nothing usable today; gaps and assumptions will be disclosed alongside the first reading at 2026-Q3 close.

measurements.question: When a smaller or more efficient model can do the job, am I choosing it?
measurements.method: Per-request model selection extracted from agent transcripts. Tasks tagged by complexity floor; measure the share routed to lighter models where complexity floor permits.
measurements.note: Direction favorable: rising share means more work is being routed to lighter models when frontier capability isn't required.

measurements.question: What roles or hours of work am I no longer paying humans for that I would have a year ago?
measurements.method: Quarterly self-report. Roles formerly outsourced or hired for, now performed by agents. Hours and dollar deltas tracked, with year-over-year framing.

measurements.question: How concentrated is my practice in any single provider's tooling, and do I have a working fallback if access were cut?
measurements.method: Token spend per provider as a share of total. Plus a quarterly tested-fallback flag — whether a real workload was successfully run on an alternate provider in the past ninety days.
measurements.note: Last successful fallback-test: 2026-04 (a real workload routed to OpenAI for three hours). Direction unfavorable as Anthropic share continues to drift upward.

measurements.question: What fraction of model-generated code from my practice am I personally reviewing before it reaches systems that affect people?
measurements.method: Instrument the deployment pipeline. Tag commits as reviewed-line-by-line versus accepted-without-line-review. Filter for commits reaching production systems.
measurements.note: Pipeline instrumentation under development. First reading expected at 2026-Q3 close.
